message:
Last winter, we lost power for three days—and I'll be honest, I was dreading it. But something weird happened: my kids actually *asked* to play board games by flashlight instead of reaching for screens. We laughed way too hard at a terrible game of charades. My partner and I talked more than we had in weeks.

I'm not saying power outages are fun (they're not). But it got me thinking: what if we actually *practiced* some of that low-tech stuff before a storm hits? Card games, books, those old-school toys collecting dust. It's not just a backup plan—it's actually kind of nice.

Have you rediscovered any games or activities you'd forgotten about? Curious what keeps your family sane when the screens go dark.
